cases.description: Always rounds toward zero, regardless of the discarded digit
For negative numbers, 'down' means toward zero (less negative), not toward negative infinity
Negative num_digits rounds to the left of the decimal point (nearest multiple of 10 here)
cases.expected_note: Rounding 21.5 down (toward zero) to the nearest 10 gives 20 -- this is Microsoft's own documented example for ROUNDDOWN, and the direct counterpart to ROUNDUP(21.5,-1)=30

cases.description: Rounds down to 2 decimal places, discarding the remaining digits
